content.heading: The Brief
content.body: A private residence on the shores of Lake Victoria — the client's request was simple: design a home that disappears into its landscape while providing every comfort expected of a luxury property. Indoor-outdoor integration, natural materials, and a calm, unhurried atmosphere were the core directives.

content.alt: Living room looking onto the lake
content.caption: Full-height glazing dissolves the boundary between interior and lake view.

items.heading: Living Spaces
items.body: The open-plan living and dining zone faces west to capture sunset light over the water. Furnishings were custom-designed and upholstered in natural linen and leather, with stone and timber detailing throughout.

items.heading: Master Suite
items.body: The master suite occupies the entire upper level, with a private terrace and a bathroom finished in honed travertine. A freestanding bath is positioned to capture the morning lake horizon — a deliberate, unhurried luxury.

content.caption: Honed travertine and solid teak joinery in the master bathroom.

content.heading: Materials & Palette
content.body: Warm buff stone, oiled teak, hand-thrown ceramics, and custom bronze hardware form a palette that feels rooted in the East African landscape. Nothing was imported for novelty's sake — every material earns its place.
